histogram_plot.caption: Histogram of coupon_used across 5,630 customers. Orange = mean, purple = median. The gap between them confirms skew direction: mean > median ⇒ right-skewed, mean < median ⇒ left-skewed.

boxplot.caption: Box plot reads as: box = 25th–75th percentile (the middle 50%), line inside box = median, whiskers extend to 1.5 × IQR, dots beyond = outliers. Large vertical separation between the two medians = strong churn predictor. Box overlap that is still high means the feature alone does NOT distinguish the two groups.

mann_whitney_u.narration: Mann-Whitney U = 2,186,070. Not statistically significant (p = 0.449). This non-parametric test asks whether churn and retained customers have the same distribution for this feature.

cohens_d.narration: Cohen's d = +0.02 — negligible effect size. Values are higher in churners (positive shift).

outliers.narration: 629 outliers by the IQR rule (11.2% of rows). p99 = 9.00, max = 16.00. Severity: substantial. The long tail is material. Recommended: log1p transform, OR cap at the 99th percentile inside the preprocessing pipeline. Decision deferred to Stage 3.2 (different treatment per model family may be appropriate).

narrative.distribution: Distribution is strongly right-skewed — most values clustered on the low end with a long right-side tail (skewness = +2.55). Mean (1.67) > median (1.00) — confirms the right skew.
narrative.boxplot: Churned median (1.00) is 0.00 lower than retained median (1.00). No meaningful separation — this feature alone does not distinguish churners from retained customers.
narrative.separation: Mann-Whitney U = 2,186,070. Not statistically significant (p = 0.449). This non-parametric test asks whether churn and retained customers have the same distribution for this feature. Cohen's d = +0.02 — negligible effect size. Values are higher in churners (positive shift).
narrative.outliers: 629 outliers by the IQR rule (11.2% of rows). p99 = 9.00, max = 16.00. Severity: substantial. The long tail is material. Recommended: log1p transform, OR cap at the 99th percentile inside the preprocessing pipeline. Decision deferred to Stage 3.2 (different treatment per model family may be appropriate).

recommendation.outlier_action: consider p99 cap or log transform in Stage 3.2
recommendation.rationale: Signal: negligible (|Cohen's d| = 0.02, p = 0.4491). Transform: log1p — heavy skew (skewness = +2.55). Outlier handling: consider p99 cap or log transform in Stage 3.2.
